questions.question: What does CSS Flexbox stand for?
questions.explanation: Flexbox stands for Flexible Box Layout, a one-dimensional layout model in CSS.

questions.question: Which CSS property creates a flex container?
questions.explanation: display: flex (or display: inline-flex) creates a flex container.

questions.question: Flexbox is a ______ layout model.
questions.explanation: Flexbox is a one-dimensional layout model, for rows OR columns, not both.

questions.question: What are the direct children of a flex container called?
questions.explanation: Direct children of a flex container are called flex items.

questions.question: Which axis is the primary axis in Flexbox?
questions.explanation: The main axis is the primary axis along which flex items are laid out.

questions.question: What is the default direction of the main axis?
questions.explanation: By default, the main axis runs left to right (horizontal).

questions.question: Which property controls the direction of the main axis?
questions.explanation: flex-direction controls whether items are laid out in a row or column.

questions.question: What is the difference between display: flex and display: inline-flex?
options: inline-flex makes the container behave like an inline element
inline-flex is only for inline elements
questions.explanation: display: inline-flex makes the flex container behave like an inline element while keeping its children as flex items.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a flex container property?
questions.explanation: flex-grow is a flex item property, not a container property.

questions.question: What happens when an element has display: flex applied?
options: The element becomes a block-level flex container
questions.explanation: The element becomes a block-level flex container, and its direct children become flex items.

questions.question: Which browsers support Flexbox?
options: All modern browsers, with partial IE 10/11 support
questions.explanation: Flexbox is supported in all modern browsers, with partial support in IE 10 (with prefixes) and IE 11.

questions.question: The axis perpendicular to the main axis is called the:
questions.explanation: The cross axis runs perpendicular to the main axis.

questions.question: Flexbox is best used for:
options: One-dimensional layouts like navigation bars or centering
questions.explanation: Flexbox excels at one-dimensional layouts: rows OR columns.

questions.question: What CSS value can be used with display to create a flex container that behaves like inline-block?
questions.explanation: display: inline-flex creates an inline-level flex container.

questions.question: A flex layout has how many axes?
questions.explanation: Flexbox has two axes: the main axis and the cross axis.

questions.question: Which of the following is a flex item property?
questions.explanation: order is a flex item property that controls the visual order of items.

questions.question: What should you use instead of margins for spacing flex items?
questions.explanation: The gap property is the recommended way to add spacing between flex items.

questions.question: When using the order property on flex items, what accessibility concern exists?
options: Screen readers follow visual order, not DOM order
Screen readers follow DOM order, not visual order
questions.explanation: The order property changes visual order but screen readers still follow DOM order.

questions.question: Flexbox properties can be categorized into:
questions.explanation: Flexbox properties are divided into flex container properties and flex item properties.

questions.question: Which layout method came before Flexbox and was commonly used with floats?
questions.explanation: Before Flexbox, developers commonly used float-based layouts with clearfix hacks.
